# bus

<a name="MessageBus"></a>
## type MessageBus

MessageBus implements a channel\-based publish/subscribe message bus.

	type MessageBus struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="New"></a>
### func New

	func New(cfg *Config, logger *slog.Logger) *MessageBus

New creates a new MessageBus.

<a name="MessageBus.Close"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Close

	func (b *MessageBus) Close()

Close shuts down the message bus and closes all subscriber channels.

<a name="MessageBus.Publish"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Publish

	func (b *MessageBus) Publish(topic string, msg *models.BusMessage) int

Publish sends a message to all subscribers of the topic. It also publishes to wildcard subscribers \(e.g., "agent.\*" matches "agent.status"\).

<a name="MessageBus.Request"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Request

	func (b *MessageBus) Request(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg *models.BusMessage) (*models.BusMessage, error)

Request sends a message and waits for a reply.

<a name="MessageBus.Stats"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Stats

	func (b *MessageBus) Stats() map[string]int

Stats returns current bus statistics.

<a name="MessageBus.Subscribe"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Subscribe

	func (b *MessageBus) Subscribe(id, topic string) *Subscriber

Subscribe creates a subscription to a topic. The topic can contain wildcards: "agent.\*" matches "agent.status", "agent.error".

<a name="MessageBus.Unsubscribe"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Unsubscribe

	func (b *MessageBus) Unsubscribe(sub *Subscriber)

Unsubscribe removes a subscription.

<a name="MessageCallback"></a>
## type MessageCallback

MessageCallback is invoked when a message arrives on a subscribed topic.

	type MessageCallback func(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg any)

<a name="Subscriber"></a>
## type Subscriber

Subscriber represents a channel that receives messages.

	type Subscriber struct {
	    ID      string
	    Topic   string
	    Channel chan *models.BusMessage
	}

<a name="SubscriptionHandler"></a>
## type SubscriptionHandler

SubscriptionHandler manages bus subscriptions with automatic lifecycle management. It handles subscribe/dispatch/teardown to eliminate duplicated handler boilerplate.

CORE\-6 FIX: Track all subscribers so Stop\(\) can unsubscribe from each one instead of relying only on per\-goroutine defer. Without this list, if the context is cancelled but a goroutine never reaches its defer \(e.g., due to channel read blocking\), the subscription leaks.

	type SubscriptionHandler struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

NewSubscriptionHandler creates a new handler

<a name="SubscriptionHandler.Start"></a>
### func \(\*SubscriptionHandler\) Start

	func (h *SubscriptionHandler) Start(parentCtx context.Context)

Start begins listening to all subscribed topics

<a name="SubscriptionHandler.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*SubscriptionHandler\) Stop

	func (h *SubscriptionHandler) Stop()

Stop gracefully shuts down all subscription goroutines
